steps.title: Rearrange to standard form and identify coefficients
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side of the equation:

$$x^2 + 5x - 47 = 0$$

From this standard form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$, we have $a = 1$, $b = 5$, and $c = -47$.

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Use the quadratic formula:

$$x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$$

Substitute $a = 1$, $b = 5$, $c = -47$:

$$x = \frac{-5 \pm \sqrt{25 - 4(1)(-47)}}{2}$$

Simplify the discriminant: $25 + 188 = 213$

$$x = \frac{-5 \pm \sqrt{213}}{2}$$

steps.title: Compare forms to find m and q, then add
steps.content:
The problem states the solutions have the form $\frac{m+\sqrt{q}}{2}$ and $\frac{m-\sqrt{q}}{2}$.

Our result $\frac{-5 \pm \sqrt{213}}{2}$ matches this pattern exactly:

$m = -5$ and $q = 213$

Therefore: $m + q = -5 + 213 = 208$
